title: tectaria pluriseriata
intro: Tectaria pluriseriata, also known as the Indian fern, is a plant species that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in India and Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. Tectaria pluriseriata can be prepared as an herbal tea or decoction, and it is also used in the form of ointments and creams to treat skin conditions and wounds.
